data.title: Israel's Military Operations in Iran and Lebanon to Proceed as Planned, Israeli Official Says
data.description: Israel's military operations in Iran and Lebanon will continue as planned despite ongoing negotiations, according to Brigadier General Effi Defrin, an Israeli military spokesperson. According to Jin10, Defrin emphasized that the operations are proceeding according to established plans, indicating no changes despite diplomatic discussions. The statement underscores Israel's commitment to its military strategy in the region, reflecting ongoing tensions and strategic priorities.

data.title: Republican Party Considers Dallas for Midterm Convention
data.description: The Republican Party is evaluating Dallas as a potential venue for its midterm convention. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the strategic move as U.S. President Donald Trump seeks to boost voter enthusiasm before the elections that will decide congressional control for the remaining two years of his presidency.

data.title: Investors Flock to Fast-Growing Private Firms Amid IPO Anticipation
data.description: Investors are eagerly seeking opportunities to invest in rapidly expanding private companies such as SpaceX and OpenAI, despite potential legal and financial risks associated with these ventures. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the growing interest in these firms as they prepare for potential public offerings. The rush to secure stakes in these companies underscores the anticipation of significant returns once they go public. As these companies continue to grow, investors are weighing the risks and rewards of early involvement in their financial journeys.

data.title: AI TRENDS | OpenAI to Discontinue Sora Video Platform
data.description: OpenAI has announced plans to discontinue its Sora video platform application, according to The Wall Street Journal. The platform, which gained significant attention upon its release last year, has since faded from public view. According to Jin10, the decision to halt the application comes as OpenAI shifts its focus to other projects and priorities within the AI sector. The move reflects the company's ongoing strategy to streamline its offerings and concentrate on areas with greater potential for impact and innovation.

data.title: Lido: Revenue last year was $40.5 million, a 23% decrease year-over-year.
data.description: Lido reported total revenue of $40.5 million last year, a 23% decrease year-over-year, compared to $52.4 million the previous year. Gross revenue declined by 18.2%, attributed to net staking outflows due to user withdrawals and a drop in staking rewards. Lido noted structural changes in Ethereum staking in 2025, including network APR compression, a shift of funds from Simple LST to exchange and institutional staking, and increased competition shrinking its leading market share. The protocol indicated its market share is shrinking, with Simple LST's share of total staking continuing to decline. Lido stated it is evaluating an LDO buyback program, potentially launching in the second quarter of this year, intending to use protocol-generated staking rewards to purchase LDO on the open market and invest it in LDO and wstETH liquidity positions held by the DAO. The protocol laid off 15% of its staff last August to ensure long-term sustainability. Lido stated its 2025 goal is to expand beyond its core staking products to offer new services to institutional investors and users seeking higher returns. It disclosed that WisdomTree, with $140 billion in assets under management, launched an Ethereum-based ETP in Europe and provides staking rewards through the decentralized Lido protocol. (The Block)

data.title: Uber Supports Multiple Candidates in California Governor Race
data.description: Uber Technologies is supporting three candidates in the California governor race, reflecting its strategic approach to a state known for influencing policy and technology trends. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Uber's decision to diversify its political support in this key state. The move underscores the company's interest in maintaining favorable conditions for its operations amid evolving regulatory landscapes.

data.title: BNY Mellon CEO Predicts Financial Institutions to Propel Crypto Adoption
data.description: BNY Mellon CEO Robin Vince has expressed confidence that major financial institutions will spearhead the next wave of cryptocurrency adoption. According to NS3.AI, Vince believes that these institutions will play a crucial role in integrating digital assets with the wider financial system. He highlighted tokenization as a potential area of growth, particularly in markets that currently face inefficiencies, such as loans and real estate. Vince noted that the broader transformation of the financial landscape through digital assets is expected to be a gradual process, spanning 5, 10, or even 15 years, contingent upon advancements in technology, regulatory frameworks, and active participation from various stakeholders.

data.title: Iran’s Nuclear Organization: Ammunition Hits Near Bushehr Nuclear Plant Tonight
data.description: Iran’s Nuclear Organization reported that around 9 PM tonight, an ammunition struck near the Bushehr nuclear power plant. According to Jin10, the incident occurred in the vicinity of the facility, raising concerns about the safety and security of the nuclear site. Details regarding the source or impact of the ammunition are yet to be disclosed. The Bushehr plant, located in southern Iran, is a significant component of the country's nuclear infrastructure. Authorities are expected to investigate the incident further to assess any potential damage or threats to the plant's operations.

data.title: SEC Clarifies Jurisdiction Amid Calls for Regulatory Reform
data.description: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) is taking steps to clarify its jurisdictional boundaries within existing law. The SEC posted on X that as lawmakers deliberate on broader reforms to prevent rogue regulation, the agency is committed to providing clarity on its jurisdiction. This statement was made during opening remarks by SEC Commissioner Paul S. Atkins at a Blockworks event. The SEC's efforts come amid ongoing discussions about the need for regulatory reform to ensure clear and effective oversight.

data.title: BNY Mellon CEO Robin Vince: Large banks will drive the next phase of crypto adoption.
data.description: BNY Mellon CEO Robin Vince stated at the Digital Asset Summit in New York that the next phase of crypto adoption will depend on large financial institutions, with banks connecting traditional finance with the digital asset ecosystem. Vince mentioned that BNY Mellon already offers digital asset custody services and emphasized that tokenization is a key focus, including creating new digital share classes for money market funds and issuing existing products in tokenized form. He also pointed out that sectors such as lending and real estate are likely to benefit first from tokenization. Vince stressed that trust and regulation will affect the speed of industry development, stating that a clear regulatory framework and "clear rules" are needed. He added that while the US GENIUS Act has passed, the revised Digital Asset Market Clarity Act is still under development, with the draft's treatment of stablecoin yields remaining controversial. The latest compromise allows rewards related to user activity but does not allow interest payments on stablecoin balances. He also stated that institutional participation still depends on security and regulation, and that this process will take 5 to 15 years. Morgan Stanley's Amy Oldenburg stated that banks' expansion into the crypto space is not driven by hype but rather by years of infrastructure development. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Petrobras Takes Steps to Manage Energy Inflation Amid Election Year
data.description: Latin America's largest oil producer, Petrobras, is taking measures to address energy inflation linked to ongoing conflicts, particularly during an election year. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that these efforts align with government strategies aimed at protecting consumers. However, there are concerns that such actions might lead to diesel shortages, potentially impacting the market and consumer access. The company's approach reflects broader governmental priorities in managing economic pressures during politically sensitive times.

data.title: Amazon Enters Humanoid Market with Fauna Robotics Acquisition
data.description: Amazon has acquired Fauna Robotics, a startup based in New York, marking its entry into the growing consumer humanoid market. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting this move as part of Amazon's strategy to expand its technological capabilities and product offerings. The acquisition reflects a broader trend among major tech companies to invest in robotics and artificial intelligence, aiming to enhance consumer experiences and operational efficiencies. This development positions Amazon alongside other technology giants exploring the potential of humanoid robots in various applications.

data.title: U.S. Justice Department Lacks Evidence of Misconduct in Fed Chair Powell Investigation
data.description: According to Jin10, a report from The Washington Post reveals that a senior aide to U.S. Department of Justice official Jeanine Pirro admitted during a closed-door hearing this month that the department has no evidence of misconduct by Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell. The investigation was focused on the renovation costs of the Federal Reserve building. The admission highlights the absence of incriminating evidence against Powell in the ongoing criminal investigation.

data.title: Canadian Borrowers Face Refinancing Challenges Amid Economic Shifts
data.description: Canada's top banking regulator has indicated that approximately 150,000 borrowers in the country may encounter difficulties refinancing their mortgages over the next two years. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the challenges posed by declining home values and rising interest rates. These economic factors are expected to impact homeowners' ability to secure favorable refinancing terms, potentially leading to financial strain for many. The regulator's warning underscores the broader economic pressures facing the housing market in Canada, as borrowers navigate a shifting financial landscape.

data.title: Qatari Billionaire Invests in German Robotics Startup
data.description: Sheikh Hamad bin Jassim Al Thani, a prominent billionaire from the Qatari royal family, has participated in the latest funding round for Neura Robotics, a German startup. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the involvement of Inc. alongside Sheikh Hamad in supporting the company's growth. Neura Robotics is focused on advancing its technological capabilities and expanding its market presence. The investment is expected to bolster the startup's development in the competitive robotics industry.

data.title: BNB Chain and YZi Labs Launch Developer Roadshow at NYU
data.description: BNB Chain has announced the commencement of its developer roadshow in collaboration with YZi Labs. YZi Labs posted on X that the event is starting at New York University, aiming to engage with student developers and innovators. The roadshow is designed to foster connections and inspire creativity among emerging tech talents. Participants will have the opportunity to explore new technologies and collaborate on innovative projects. This initiative marks a significant step in promoting blockchain education and development within academic institutions.

data.title: U.S. Defense Secretary Hegseth: We Negotiate with Ammunition
data.description: U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has emphasized the strategic importance of military readiness in diplomatic negotiations. According to Jin10, Hegseth stated that the United States leverages its ammunition and military capabilities as a key component in discussions with other nations. This approach underscores the country's commitment to maintaining a strong defense posture while engaging in international diplomacy. Hegseth's remarks highlight the ongoing balance between military preparedness and diplomatic efforts in U.S. foreign policy.

data.title: Russian Citizen Sentenced for Role in Ransomware Attacks
data.description: A U.S. court has sentenced Russian citizen Aleksei Volkov to 81 months in prison for his involvement in aiding ransomware groups, resulting in over $9 million in losses across the United States. According to NS3.AI, Volkov pleaded guilty to six charges across two indictments and acknowledged receiving a portion of the cryptocurrency ransom payments. Additionally, the court mandated nearly $9.2 million in restitution to identified victims and the forfeiture of equipment used in the criminal activities.

data.title: BMO Enables 24/7 Tokenized Cash Settlement via CME and Google Cloud
data.description: BMO has announced that its clients can now convert dollars into tokenized cash and deposits on CME and Google Cloud's Universal Ledger. According to NS3.AI, this integration allows for 24/7 settlement of margin, collateral, and B2B payments, extending institutional access to tokenized settlement beyond traditional trading hours. BMO has described this rollout as a significant milestone in the adoption of institutional tokenization infrastructure.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Apple Tests Standalone Siri App Amid AI Overhaul
data.description: Apple is reportedly testing a standalone application for its Siri voice assistant, as part of a comprehensive artificial intelligence update. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that the tech giant is also developing a new feature called “Ask Siri,” which is designed to function seamlessly across its software ecosystem. This move is seen as part of Apple's broader strategy to enhance its AI capabilities and improve user interaction with its devices. The standalone app and the new feature are expected to provide users with more flexibility and improved functionality when using Siri. Apple's efforts reflect the growing emphasis on AI technology within the tech industry, as companies strive to offer more intuitive and efficient digital assistants.
